title: Historic agreement between US and Iran on nuclear program and sanctions
summary: The memorandum of understanding contemplates the resumption of Iranian oil exports, the end of the naval blockade, and a $300 billion reconstruction plan.

title: G7 summit concludes with agreements on critical minerals, artificial intelligence, and child protection
summary: Group leaders agreed to reduce reliance on a single country for key minerals and regulate artificial intelligence, showing unity despite differences.

title: Captain of Shiretoko boat sentenced to 5 years in prison for accident with 8 deaths
summary: The boat captain was sentenced for negligence in the shipwreck that caused eight deaths, generating outrage among relatives.

title: Ruling party proposes reducing consumption tax on food to 1% to make it effectively zero
summary: The proposal includes a reduced rate and a direct subsidy to offset the effective tax, generating political debate.

title: Former national volleyball coach accused of cannabis possession
summary: Shunichiro Sato, a member of the national team, was prosecuted for violating the narcotics law after marijuana was found during training.

title: School principal sentenced to 2 years in prison (suspended) for student's death in swimming class
summary: The Kochi court found the principal guilty of negligence in the death of a fourth-grade child during a swimming lesson.

title: Mother sues state for death of her 16-year-old son after arrest and lack of indictment
summary: The family accuses the police of an illegal investigation that led to the young man's death, who died from unclear causes.

title: Yohei Kono, former foreign minister, dies and delegation to China is reduced
summary: The Japan Trade Board will send a smaller delegation to China after Kono's death, with no high-level meetings planned.

title: Seven-Eleven launches onigiri with simpler packaging to reduce costs
summary: The convenience store chain redesigns its rice balls, removing nori and using fewer colors to save on packaging.

title: Trade Commission to investigate 150,000 companies for oil cost pass-through
summary: The antitrust authority will conduct an urgent study to verify whether companies are passing on the oil price increase to consumers.

title: Nikkei index reaches all-time high, surpassing 70,000 points
summary: The Tokyo Stock Exchange closed at a new record for the third consecutive session, boosted by the US-Iran agreement.

title: Japan tourism falls for second consecutive month with sharp drop in Chinese visitors
summary: In May, the number of foreigners visiting Japan was lower than last year, especially due to a 60% drop in Chinese tourists.

title: Russian artist critical of Putin shot dead in Poland
summary: A well-known artist opposed to the Russian government was shot dead in Poland and two people were arrested as suspects.

title: Yomiuri Giants sign pitcher Shinnosuke Ogasawara
summary: The Japanese baseball club confirmed the signing of the former Dragons pitcher, who was playing in the Nationals' minor leagues.

title: Japan's emperor and empress visit Netherlands, pay tribute to war dead
summary: The imperial couple laid flowers at the monument to Dutch soldiers and participated in events with the local community during their state visit.

title: Rakuten appoints Yoshii as new manager after owner Mikuni's offer
summary: The baseball team chose former pitcher Yoshii as manager, confirming the offer that Mikuni called a 'bet'.

title: Shinkansen private cars to be called 'Supreme Class' and cost up to 42,000 yen
summary: The new individual cabins on bullet trains from Tokyo to Nagoya and Osaka will be available from October with fares between 32,660 and 42,000 yen.

title: Court declares Lower House election constitutional despite vote inequality
summary: The high court rejected the challenge alleging that the difference in vote value violated the Constitution.

title: Negotiations to merge three opposition political parties stall after five months
summary: Centrist leader Ogawa warns that lack of progress limits the opposition's ability to compete.

title: Former investment executive arrested for allegedly embezzling 50 million yen
summary: The Osaka Public Prosecutors Office detained the former head of an investment firm for diverting investor funds for personal use.
